ogTitle: Old Plains vs Ongerup: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Old Plains and Ongerup.
heading: Old Plains vs Ongerup — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Old Plains, Western Australia and Ongerup, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Ongerup has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Old Plains (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Old Plains or Ongerup safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Old Plains has a flood risk score of 55/100, while Ongerup scores 35/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Old Plains scores 40/100 for bushfire risk. Ongerup scores 60/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Old Plains or Ongerup?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Ongerup has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.48% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
